pathway.title: Chest Pain ED Evaluation Pathway
pathway.description: Any chest pain requiring urgent emergency evaluation — rule out ACS, PE, aortic dissection.

steps.action: 12-lead ECG within 10 minutes of arrival
Troponin I or T × 2 (at 0h and 3h)
CBC, BMP, coagulation studies, BNP
steps.rationale: Assess for heart failure, metabolic causes, coagulation baseline

steps.action: Chest X-ray (PA + lateral)
Aspirin 325mg PO STAT (if ACS suspected, no contraindication)
steps.rationale: Antiplatelet — reduces platelet aggregation in ACS

steps.action: CT-PA if PERC positive or Wells score ≥2 (PE evaluation)
Continuous cardiac monitoring, O2 saturation monitoring, IV access
steps.rationale: Hemodynamic monitoring and immediate intervention readiness

steps.action: Cardiology consult if ECG changes, elevated troponin, or hemodynamic instability

contraindications: Aspirin: active GI bleeding, allergy, anticoagulation with recent hemorrhagic stroke
escalationCriteria: STEMI on ECG → cath lab activation
outcomeGoals: ACS ruled in or out within 3h

labsOrdered: CT-PA if PERC positive or Wells score ≥2 (PE evaluation)
medicationsPrescribed: Aspirin 325mg PO STAT (if ACS suspected, no contraindication)
referralsPlaced: Cardiology consult if ECG changes, elevated troponin, or hemodynamic instability
contraindications: Aspirin: active GI bleeding, allergy, anticoagulation with recent hemorrhagic stroke
escalationCriteria: STEMI on ECG → cath lab activation

data.text:
DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS

Your symptoms can be managed at home. Rest, stay hydrated, and take OTC medications as needed.

RETURN PRECAUTIONS — Return to care immediately if you develop:
• Chest pain or severe difficulty breathing
• High fever (>39°C / 102°F)
• Confusion, severe headache, or stiff neck
• Severe worsening of any symptom

This assessment is for triage guidance only and does not replace in-person medical evaluation.

data.message: I have a fever, stiff neck, and worst headache of my life

pathway.title: Viral Fever / URTI Pathway
pathway.description: Uncomplicated fever <5 days without red flags — likely viral etiology.

steps.action: Maintain oral hydration — 2L/day minimum
steps.rationale: Fever causes increased insensible fluid losses

steps.action: Acetaminophen 500–1000mg q6h PRN (temp >101°F / discomfort)
Ibuprofen 400mg q6h with food PRN (alternating with acetaminophen)
steps.rationale: NSAID antipyretic — can alternate for better fever control
steps.timing: PRN, avoid in renal impairment, GI disease

steps.action: Light clothing, cool environment, tepid sponge bath if very high fever
Monitor temperature q4h — return if >103.5°F or not improving in 5 days
steps.rationale: Detect worsening or secondary bacterial infection

steps.action: Return if fever persists >5 days, rash develops, stiff neck, or confusion
steps.timing: Day 5 or sooner if red flags

contraindications: Aspirin: children/adolescents (Reye syndrome)
Ibuprofen: GI bleed history, CKD, pregnancy third trimester

medicationsPrescribed: Acetaminophen 500–1000mg q6h PRN (temp >101°F / discomfort)
Ibuprofen 400mg q6h with food PRN (alternating with acetaminophen)
followupScheduled: Return if fever persists >5 days, rash develops, stiff neck, or confusion
contraindications: Aspirin: children/adolescents (Reye syndrome)
Ibuprofen: GI bleed history, CKD, pregnancy third trimester

data.message: severe sore throat with fever, white patches, and swollen glands for 4 days

result.explanation: The symptom pattern of fever associated with sore throat is consistent with the ranked differential diagnoses based on clinical case memory and probabilistic inference.
reasoning_path: L2 Similarity: 5 cases found, top Dx: strep_pharyngitis
L3 Bayes: top Dx pneumonia (10%), entropy=1.14

pathway.title: Bacterial Pharyngitis (Strep) Pathway
pathway.description: Centor score ≥3 — treat empirically or after rapid strep test.

steps.action: Rapid Strep Antigen Test (RADT)
Amoxicillin 500mg PO TID × 10 days (adult)
steps.timing: Start same day if RADT positive or Centor ≥4

steps.action: Azithromycin 500mg PO Day 1, then 250mg × 4 days (penicillin allergy)
Ibuprofen 400mg q6h PRN or Acetaminophen 650mg q6h PRN
Rest, hydration, warm liquids, throat lozenges
Return if symptoms not improving after 48–72h of antibiotics
steps.rationale: Rule out peritonsillar abscess or resistant organism

steps.action: Monitor for rash (scarlet fever) or difficulty breathing

contraindications: Concurrent mononucleosis (avoid amoxicillin → rash risk)

medicationsPrescribed: Amoxicillin 500mg PO TID × 10 days (adult)
Azithromycin 500mg PO Day 1, then 250mg × 4 days (penicillin allergy)
Ibuprofen 400mg q6h PRN or Acetaminophen 650mg q6h PRN
followupScheduled: Return if symptoms not improving after 48–72h of antibiotics
contraindications: Concurrent mononucleosis (avoid amoxicillin → rash risk)

data.message: productive cough with green sputum, fever 38.5, shortness of breath, chest pain

result.explanation: The symptom pattern of fever, cough, productive_cough associated with cough is consistent with the ranked differential diagnoses based on clinical case memory and probabilistic inference.
reasoning_path: L2 Similarity: 5 cases found, top Dx: pneumonia
L3 Bayes: top Dx pneumonia (33%), entropy=1.16

pathway.title: Acute Viral URTI / Cough Pathway
pathway.description: Cough <3 weeks without red flags — likely viral, supportive care.

steps.action: Honey 1–2 tsp PRN (adults), warm beverages, humidifier
Guaifenesin 400mg q4h PRN (expectorant for productive cough)
Dextromethorphan 15–30mg q6-8h PRN (dry cough)
Ibuprofen or Acetaminophen PRN for fever/myalgias
Hand hygiene, mask use around vulnerable individuals, rest
Return if cough >3 weeks, hemoptysis, fever >5 days, or SOB
steps.rationale: Rule out pneumonia, pertussis, or sinusitis

contraindications: Dextromethorphan: MAOI use, certain SSRIs (serotonin syndrome risk)

medicationsPrescribed: Guaifenesin 400mg q4h PRN (expectorant for productive cough)
Dextromethorphan 15–30mg q6-8h PRN (dry cough)
Ibuprofen or Acetaminophen PRN for fever/myalgias
followupScheduled: Return if cough >3 weeks, hemoptysis, fever >5 days, or SOB
contraindications: Dextromethorphan: MAOI use, certain SSRIs (serotonin syndrome risk)

data.message: crushing chest pain radiating to my left arm, diaphoresis, shortness of breath

alerts: 🔒 [SR-001] Radiation to left arm is a cardinal sign of ACS. Life-threatening if missed.
🔒 [SR-002] Diaphoresis with chest pain strongly suggests acute myocardial infarction.

data.message: mild sore throat for 1 day, no fever, no difficulty swallowing

result.explanation: The symptom pattern of fever associated with sore throat is consistent with the ranked differential diagnoses based on clinical case memory and probabilistic inference.
reasoning_path: L2 Similarity: 5 cases found, top Dx: strep_pharyngitis
L3 Bayes: top Dx pneumonia (10%), entropy=1.14

pathway.title: Bacterial Pharyngitis (Strep) Pathway
pathway.description: Centor score ≥3 — treat empirically or after rapid strep test.

steps.action: Rapid Strep Antigen Test (RADT)
Amoxicillin 500mg PO TID × 10 days (adult)
steps.timing: Start same day if RADT positive or Centor ≥4

steps.action: Azithromycin 500mg PO Day 1, then 250mg × 4 days (penicillin allergy)
Ibuprofen 400mg q6h PRN or Acetaminophen 650mg q6h PRN
Rest, hydration, warm liquids, throat lozenges
Return if symptoms not improving after 48–72h of antibiotics
steps.rationale: Rule out peritonsillar abscess or resistant organism

steps.action: Monitor for rash (scarlet fever) or difficulty breathing

contraindications: Concurrent mononucleosis (avoid amoxicillin → rash risk)

medicationsPrescribed: Amoxicillin 500mg PO TID × 10 days (adult)
Azithromycin 500mg PO Day 1, then 250mg × 4 days (penicillin allergy)
Ibuprofen 400mg q6h PRN or Acetaminophen 650mg q6h PRN
followupScheduled: Return if symptoms not improving after 48–72h of antibiotics
contraindications: Concurrent mononucleosis (avoid amoxicillin → rash risk)

data.message: productive cough with fever and shortness of breath

result.explanation: The symptom pattern of fever, cough, productive_cough associated with cough is consistent with the ranked differential diagnoses based on clinical case memory and probabilistic inference.
reasoning_path: L2 Similarity: 5 cases found, top Dx: pneumonia
L3 Bayes: top Dx pneumonia (33%), entropy=1.16

pathway.title: Acute Viral URTI / Cough Pathway
pathway.description: Cough <3 weeks without red flags — likely viral, supportive care.

steps.action: Honey 1–2 tsp PRN (adults), warm beverages, humidifier
Guaifenesin 400mg q4h PRN (expectorant for productive cough)
Dextromethorphan 15–30mg q6-8h PRN (dry cough)
Ibuprofen or Acetaminophen PRN for fever/myalgias
Hand hygiene, mask use around vulnerable individuals, rest
Return if cough >3 weeks, hemoptysis, fever >5 days, or SOB
steps.rationale: Rule out pneumonia, pertussis, or sinusitis

contraindications: Dextromethorphan: MAOI use, certain SSRIs (serotonin syndrome risk)

medicationsPrescribed: Guaifenesin 400mg q4h PRN (expectorant for productive cough)

followupScheduled: Return if cough >3 weeks, hemoptysis, fever >5 days, or SOB
contraindications: Dextromethorphan: MAOI use, certain SSRIs (serotonin syndrome risk)

sudden worst headache of my life with stiff neck and fever

Red Flags Detected: SR-006, SR-007

Disposition: ER NOW

data.text:
DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS

⚠ EMERGENCY: Go to the nearest Emergency Room or call 911 immediately. Do not delay.

RETURN PRECAUTIONS — Return to care immediately if you develop:
• Chest pain or severe difficulty breathing
• High fever (>39°C / 102°F)
• Confusion, severe headache, or stiff neck
• Severe worsening of any symptom

This assessment is for triage guidance only and does not replace in-person medical evaluation.

result.explanation: The symptom pattern of fever, cough, productive_cough associated with cough is consistent with the ranked differential diagnoses based on clinical case memory and probabilistic inference.
reasoning_path: L2 Similarity: 5 cases found, top Dx: pneumonia
L3 Bayes: top Dx pneumonia (33%), entropy=1.16

pathway.title: Acute Viral URTI / Cough Pathway
pathway.description: Cough <3 weeks without red flags — likely viral, supportive care.

steps.action: Honey 1–2 tsp PRN (adults), warm beverages, humidifier
Guaifenesin 400mg q4h PRN (expectorant for productive cough)
Dextromethorphan 15–30mg q6-8h PRN (dry cough)
Ibuprofen or Acetaminophen PRN for fever/myalgias
Hand hygiene, mask use around vulnerable individuals, rest
Return if cough >3 weeks, hemoptysis, fever >5 days, or SOB
steps.rationale: Rule out pneumonia, pertussis, or sinusitis

contraindications: Dextromethorphan: MAOI use, certain SSRIs (serotonin syndrome risk)
